Boat Pose (Navasana).benefits: Strengthens core and hip flexors, improves balance and digestion.
Boat Pose (Navasana).instructions: Sit on the floor, lift legs and torso to form a V, extend arms forward, balance on sit bones.

Bound Angle Pose (Baddha Konasana).benefits: Opens hips and groin, improves flexibility and circulation, stretches inner thighs.
Bound Angle Pose (Baddha Konasana).instructions: Sit, bend knees, press soles together, let knees fall outward, keep spine straight.

Bow Pose (Dhanurasana).benefits: Strengthens back muscles, improves spinal flexibility, opens chest and hips.
Bow Pose (Dhanurasana).instructions: Lie on belly, bend knees, reach back to grasp ankles, lift chest and thighs while pulling feet back.

Bridge Pose (Setu Bandha Sarvangasana).benefits: Stretches chest, neck and spine, strengthens legs and lower back.
Bridge Pose (Setu Bandha Sarvangasana).instructions: Lie on back with knees bent, lift hips upward, clasp hands under body.

Camel Pose (Ustrasana).benefits: Opens chest, shoulders and hip flexors, improves posture and spinal flexibility.
Camel Pose (Ustrasana).instructions: Kneel, place hands on lower back or heels, press hips forward and arch back, open chest.

Cat Pose (Marjariasana).benefits: Improves spinal mobility, stretches back and neck, relieves tension.
Cat Pose (Marjariasana).instructions: From tabletop, exhale arch spine upward, tuck chin and tailbone, then inhale to neutral.

Cobra Pose (Bhujangasana).benefits: Increases spinal flexibility, strengthens back, opens chest.
Cobra Pose (Bhujangasana).instructions: Lie on belly, hands under shoulders, inhale lift chest while pressing pelvis down.

Cow Pose (Bitilasana).benefits: Improves spinal mobility, stretches chest and torso, coordinates breath with movement.
Cow Pose (Bitilasana).instructions: From hands-and-knees, inhale drop belly, lift chest and tailbone upward.

Crane Pose (Bakasana).benefits: Builds arm strength, improves balance and focus.
Crane Pose (Bakasana).instructions: Squat, place hands on floor, lean forward, lift feet and balance knees on upper arms.

Downward Facing Dog (Adho Mukha Svanasan.benefits: Stretches hamstrings, calves and spine, strengthens arms and shoulders.
Downward Facing Dog (Adho Mukha Svanasan.instructions: From tabletop, lift hips up and back to form an inverted V shape.

Easy Pose (Sukhasana).benefits: Calms mind, stretches knees and ankles, improves posture.
Easy Pose (Sukhasana).instructions: Sit cross‑legged, spine tall, shoulders relaxed.

Four‑Limbed Staff Pose (Chaturanga Danda.instructions: From plank, lower body while keeping elbows close to ribs.

Plank Pose (Phalakasana).instructions: From hands and toes, hold body in straight line.

Triangle Pose (Trikonasana).benefits: Stretches hips, legs and spine, improves balance.
Triangle Pose (Trikonasana).instructions: Stand wide, reach forward, hinge at hip, lower hand to shin/ankle and extend opposite arm.

Child's Pose (Balasana).benefits: Relaxes back and shoulders, calms the mind.
Child's Pose (Balasana).instructions: From tabletop, sit back on heels and stretch arms forward.

Downward Dog Split (Three‑Legged Dog).benefits: Improves balance and strength, stretches hamstrings.
Downward Dog Split (Three‑Legged Dog).instructions: From downward dog, lift one leg high and hold.

Upward Facing Dog (Urdhva Mukha Svanasan.benefits: Opens chest, strengthens back and arms.
Upward Facing Dog (Urdhva Mukha Svanasan.instructions: Lie on belly, press hands down, lift chest and legs off floor.

Warrior I (Virabhadrasana I).benefits: Strengthens legs and core, improves stability.
Warrior I (Virabhadrasana I).instructions: From standing, step back, bend front knee, lift arms overhead.

Warrior II (Virabhadrasana II).instructions: Wide stance, bend front knee, extend arms sideways.

Warrior III (Virabhadrasana III).instructions: Lean forward, lift back leg and keep body in straight line.

Garland Pose (Malasana).instructions: Squat low with feet apart, spine tall.

High Lunge (Anjaneyasana).benefits: Strengthens legs and hips, stretches hip flexors.
High Lunge (Anjaneyasana).instructions: Step one foot back, bend front knee and lift arms overhead.

Seated Forward Bend (Paschimottanasana).instructions: Sit with legs long, hinge forward from hips.

Legs Up the Wall (Viparita Karani).benefits: Relaxes legs and back, calms mind.
Legs Up the Wall (Viparita Karani).instructions: Lie on back with legs extended up against a wall.

Head‑to‑Knee Forward Bend (Janu Sirsasan.instructions: Sit, one leg straight, hinge forward over extended leg.

Side Plank (Vasisthasana).instructions: From plank, rotate onto side and lift top arm.

Crow Pose (Kakasana).instructions: Squat, place hands, lean forward and lift feet.

Half Moon Pose (Ardha Chandrasana).benefits: Improves balance and coordination, strengthens legs, stretches groin and hamstrings.
Half Moon Pose (Ardha Chandrasana).instructions: From standing, place one hand on the floor or block, lift the opposite leg, extend your top arm towards the ceiling and open your chest.

Hero Pose (Virasana).benefits: Stretches quadriceps and ankles, improves posture and circulation, calms the mind.
Hero Pose (Virasana).instructions: Kneel with knees together, sit between your feet, keep spine tall, hands resting on thighs.

Monkey Pose (Hanumanasana).benefits: Deep hamstring and hip flexor stretch, improves flexibility and leg strength.
Monkey Pose (Hanumanasana).instructions: Start in low lunge, square your hips, slide the front heel forward until legs are straight in a split position, keep torso upright.

Plow Pose (Halasana).benefits: Stretches spine, shoulders and hamstrings, stimulates nervous and digestive systems.
Plow Pose (Halasana).instructions: Lie on your back, lift legs overhead until toes touch the floor behind the head, keep hands on the back or floor for support.

Super Fish Pose (Matsyasana variation).benefits: Opens chest and throat, strengthens upper back and improves breathing.
Super Fish Pose (Matsyasana variation).instructions: Lie on your back, arch chest up, crown of head rests lightly on the floor, engage core and lift slightly for chest opening.

Table Pose (Bharmanasana).benefits: Strengthens wrists, arms, and core while stabilizing shoulders, improves posture.
Table Pose (Bharmanasana).instructions: From hands and knees, align shoulders over wrists and hips over knees, engage core, keep spine neutral.
